questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Directive Principles of State Policy:
  1. The Principles spell out the socio-economic democracy in the country
  2. The provisions contained in these Principles are not enforceable by any court
Which of the statements given below are correct?
questions.solution:
Both statements are correct:
  • Statement 1: The DPSP aim at establishing social and economic democracy, complementing the political democracy established by Fundamental Rights.
  • Statement 2: Article 37 explicitly states that these provisions are not enforceable by any court (non-justiciable).

questions.question: Which one of the following Schedules of the Constitution of India contains provisions regarding anti-defection?
questions.solution:
The Tenth Schedule was added by the 52nd Amendment Act (1985) to prevent political defections.
  • It lays down the process by which legislators may be disqualified on grounds of defection (switching parties).
  • Eighth Schedule: Languages. Fifth Schedule: Tribal areas.

questions.question: In the Constitution of India, the promotion of international peace and security is included in the
questions.solution: Article 51 of the DPSP specifically mandates that the State shall strive to promote international peace and security, maintain just and honorable relations between nations, and encourage settlement of disputes by arbitration.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. A Constitutional Government is one which places effective restrictions on individual liberty in the interest of State Authority
  2. A Constitutional Government is one which places effective restrictions on the Authority of the State in the interest of individual liberty
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Statement 2 is correct.
  • A Constitutional Government is defined by Limited Government. It places effective restrictions on the Authority of the State to ensure that individual liberties are protected.
  • Statement 1 describes an authoritarian or totalitarian view, which is the opposite of constitutionalism.

questions.question:
Which of the following are the discretionary powers given to the Governor of a State?
  1. Sending a report to the President of India for imposing the President's rule
  2. Appointing the Ministers
  3. Reserving certain bills passed by the State Legislature for consideration of the President of India
  4. Making the rules to conduct the business of the State Government
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
The Governor has two types of discretionary powers: Constitutional and Situational.
  • Sending a report (Art 356): Discretionary; based on their own assessment of the breakdown of constitutional machinery.
  • Reserving bills (Art 200): Discretionary; they can choose to send a bill to the President.
  • Appointing Ministers & Business Rules: Usually done on the advice of the Chief Minister (not discretionary).

questions.question: With reference to Indian History, the Members of the Constituent Assembly from the Provinces were
questions.option_1: Directly elected by the people of those Provinces
questions.option_2: Nominated by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League
questions.option_3: Elected by the Provincial Legislative Assemblies
questions.option_4: Selected by the Government for their expertise in constitutional matters
questions.solution:
Members of the Constituent Assembly were elected by the members of the Provincial Legislative Assemblies through the method of proportional representation by means of a single transferable vote.
  • They were not directly elected by the people of India at large (due to limited franchise at the time).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. An amendment to the Constitution of India can be initiated by an introduction of a bill in the Lok Sabha only
  2. If such an amendment seeks to make changes in the federal character of the Constitution, the amendment also requires to be ratified by the legislatures of all the States of India
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Neither statement is correct.
  • Statement 1: An amendment bill can be initiated in either House of Parliament (Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha).
  • Statement 2: Changes to the federal character require ratification by the legislatures of at least half of the States, not 'all' of them.

questions.question: 'Economic Justice' as one of the objectives of the Indian Constitution has been provided in
questions.option_2: The Preamble and the Directive Principles of State Policy
questions.option_3: The Fundamental Rights and the Directive Principles of State Policy
questions.solution:
The objective of 'Economic Justice' is found in:
  • The Preamble: Mentioning Justice—Social, Economic, and Political.
  • DPSP: Article 38 and Article 39 mandate the State to minimize inequalities in income and ensure a fair distribution of resources.

questions.question: According to the Constitution of India, which of the following are fundamental for the governance of the country?
questions.solution:
Article 37 explicitly declares that the Directive Principles of State Policy are fundamental in the governance of the country.
  • While Fundamental Rights are fundamental to the individual, DPSPs are the guiding principles for the State's governance.

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are among the Fundamental Duties, laid down in the Indian Constitution?
  1. To preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture
  2. To protect the weaker sections from social injustice
  3. To develop the scientific temper and spirit of inquiry
  4. To strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity
Select the correct answer, using the codes below:
questions.solution:
Statement 1, 3, and 4 are correct:
  • Duty 1: To value and preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture.
  • Duty 3: To develop scientific temper, humanism, and the spirit of inquiry.
  • Duty 4: To strive towards excellence in all spheres.
  • Statement 2 is incorrect: Protecting weaker sections is a Directive Principle (Article 46), not a Fundamental Duty of the citizen.

questions.question:
Consider the following provisions under the Directive Principles of State Policy as enshrined in the Constitution of India:
  1. Securing for citizens of India a uniform civil code
  2. Organizing village Panchayats
  3. Promoting cottage industries in rural areas
  4. Securing for all the workers reasonable leisure and cultural opportunities
Which of the above Gandhian Principles are reflected in the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.solution:
The Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) are categorized into Socialist, Gandhian, and Liberal-Intellectual principles.
  • Gandhian Principles: These include Organizing village Panchayats (Article 40) and Promoting cottage industries (Article 43).
  • Uniform Civil Code (Article 44): This is a Liberal-Intellectual principle.
  • Leisure and cultural opportunities (Article 43): This is a Socialist principle aimed at worker welfare.
Since only statements 2 and 3 represent Gandhian ideology, the second option is correct.

questions.question:
Which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India have a bearing on Education?
  1. Directive Principles of State Policy
  2. Rural and Urban Local Bodies
  3. Fifth Schedule
  4. Sixth Schedule
  5. Seventh Schedule
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.solution:
Education is a multifaceted subject in the Indian Constitution, and all listed provisions have a bearing on it:
  • DPSP: Article 45 (Early childhood care) and Article 46 (Education for SC/STs).
  • Local Bodies: The 73rd and 74th Amendments gave Panchayats and Municipalities responsibilities over primary and secondary education.
  • 5th & 6th Schedules: These allow for the administration of tribal areas, including the establishment of schools.
  • 7th Schedule: Education is in the Concurrent List (Entry 25), allowing both Union and States to legislate.

questions.question: Under the Constitution of India, which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?
questions.option_4: To abide by Constitution and respect its ideals
questions.solution:
Fundamental Duties (Part IV-A, Article 51A) were added by the 42nd Amendment.
  • To vote in public elections: This is a Statutory/Constitutional Right, but it is not listed as a Fundamental Duty.
  • Other options: Scientific temper, safeguarding public property, and abiding by the Constitution are all explicitly listed under Article 51A.

questions.question:
Consider the following:
  1. Right to education
  2. Right to equal access to public service
  3. Right to food
Which of the above is/are Human Right/Human Rights under 'Universal Declaration of Human Rights'?
questions.solution:
The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) is a comprehensive document covering civil, political, economic, and social rights.
  • Right to Education: Article 26 of UDHR.
  • Access to Public Service: Article 21 of UDHR.
  • Right to Food: Included as part of the 'Right to a standard of living' in Article 25 of UDHR.
Therefore, all three are recognized Human Rights globally.

questions.question: The 'Instrument of Instructions' contained in the Government of India Act 1935 have been incorporated in the Constitution of India in the year 1950 as
questions.option_3: Extent of executive power of State
questions.option_4: Conduct of business of the Government of India
questions.solution:
The 'Instrument of Instructions' were directives issued to the Governor-General and Governors under the British Raj.
  • In the 1950 Constitution, these were renamed and incorporated as the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP).
  • The only major difference is that while the Instruments were for the executive, the DPSP are for both the legislature and the executive.

questions.question: Which one of the following Constitutional Amendments states that the total number of Ministers, including the Prime Minister, in the Council of Ministers shall not exceed fifteen percent of the total number of members of the House of the People?
questions.solution:
The 91st Amendment Act (2003) was enacted to limit the size of the Council of Ministers to prevent jumbo cabinets and strengthen the anti-defection law.
  • It states that the total number of ministers (including the PM) in the Union Council of Ministers shall not exceed 15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha.
  • A similar provision applies to the States (Legislative Assembly).

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution of India contains special provisions for the administration and control of Scheduled Areas in several States?
questions.solution: The Fifth Schedule of the Constitution contains special provisions for the administration and control of 'Scheduled Areas' and 'Scheduled Tribes' in states other than Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram (which are covered under the Sixth Schedule).

questions.question: Under which one of the following Constitution Amendment Acts were four languages added to the languages under the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India, thereby raising their number to 22?
questions.solution:
The 92nd Amendment Act (2003) added four languages to the Eighth Schedule: Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, and Santhali (often remembered as 'BDMS').
  • This brought the total number of officially recognized languages in India to 22.

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are included in the Directive Principles of State Policy?
  1. Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labor
  2. Prohibition of consumption except for medicinal purposes of intoxicating drinks and of other drugs which are injurious to health
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
This question contrasts Fundamental Rights and DPSP:
  • Statement 1: This is a Fundamental Right under Article 23, not a DPSP.
  • Statement 2: This is a Directive Principle under Article 47, which mandates the State to endeavor to bring about prohibition of intoxicating drinks and drugs.

questions.question: Who among the following was the Chairman of the Union Constitution Committee of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.solution:
The Constituent Assembly had various committees to divide the workload of drafting:
  • Jawaharlal Nehru: Chairman of the Union Constitution Committee and the Union Powers Committee.
  • Dr. B.R. Ambedkar: Chairman of the Drafting Committee.
  • J.B. Kripalani: Chairman of the Fundamental Rights Sub-Committee.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. The Constitution of India has 20 parts
  2. There are 390 Articles in the Constitution of India
  3. Ninth, Tenth, Eleventh and Twelfth Schedules were added to the Constitution of India by the Constitution (Amendment) Acts
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's look at the current structure versus the original:
  • Statement 1 & 2: These are incorrect as the Constitution currently has 25 Parts and over 448 Articles (originally it had 22 Parts and 395 Articles).
  • Statement 3: Correct. The original Constitution had only 8 schedules. The 9th (1st Amendment), 10th (52nd Amendment), 11th (73rd Amendment), and 12th (74th Amendment) were all added later.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. Article 371A to 371J were inserted in the Constitution of India to meet regional demands of Nagaland, Assam, Manipur, Andhra Pradesh, Sikkim, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh and Goa
  2. Constitution of India and the United States of America envisages a dual polity (The Union and the States) but a single citizenship
  3. A naturalized citizen of India can never be deprived of his citizenship
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Statement 1 is correct: Articles 371A to 371J provide special provisions for various states to address regional imbalances and cultural uniqueness.
Statement 2 is incorrect: While the US has dual citizenship, India has a Single Citizenship despite having a dual polity (Union/States).
Statement 3 is incorrect: Naturalized citizens can be deprived of citizenship under certain circumstances (like fraud or disloyalty) as per the Citizenship Act, 1955.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements:
  1. Article 301 pertains to the Right to Property
  2. Right to Property is a legal right but not a Fundamental Right
  3. Article 300A was inserted in the Constitution of India by the Congress Government at the Centre by the 44th Constitutional Amendment
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Statement 2 is correct: The Right to Property is a Legal Right under Article 300A.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Article 301 relates to the Freedom of Trade and Commerce, not property.
Statement 3 is incorrect: The 44th Amendment (1978) was passed by the Janata Party Government (under Morarji Desai), not the Congress government.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India says, 'No child below the age of fourteen years shall be employed to work in any factory or mine or engaged in any other hazardous employment'?
questions.solution:
Article 24 of the Constitution specifically prohibits the employment of children below the age of 14 in factories, mines, or any hazardous work.
  • This is a key Fundamental Right against exploitation.
  • Article 45: Deals with early childhood education.
  • Article 330: Deals with reservation of seats for SC/STs in Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India says that the executive power of every State shall be so exercised as not to impede or prejudice the exercise of the executive power of the Union?
questions.solution: Article 257 provides for the control of the Union over States in certain cases. It specifies that the executive power of the State must be exercised in a way that does not impede the executive power of the Union. The Union can also issue directions to States for this purpose.
